channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon in crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: Israeli airstrikes have hit the headquarters of the U.N. peacekeeping force and other positions in southern Lebanon in recent days, drawing widespread condemnation from around the world.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stellantis CEO under fire from Italian lawmakers as the group grapples with financial troubles
item.description: Italian lawmakers on Friday questioned the head of troubled French-Italian automaker Stellantis over the group’s strategy, one day after it announced a management shakeup in an effort to revive sales.
item.dc:creator: Giada Zampano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkish citizens fleeing Lebanon mourn the homes and family left behind
item.description: ON BOARD THE TCG SANCAKTAR (AP) — Eyup Sabri Kirgiz gathered up his loved ones — both family and pets — and with a heavy heart left his beloved city of Beirut behind, after two weeks of deadly airstrikes that had traumatized his family.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nobel prize for A-bomb survivors' group boosts hope for Japanese seeking a nuke-free world
item.description: For many Japanese survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki and their relatives, Friday’s awarding of the Nobel Peace Prize to a Japanese organization against nuclear weapons gives them hope for a new momentum to push for a nuclear-free world.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's national airliner accused of flying weapons to Beirut airport for terror proxy Hezbollah: Report
item.description: Iran’s domestic airline, already sanctioned for support to Russia, is being accused of aiding Hezbollah by an Iranian dissident group who has called for additional sanctions to be put on the carrier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: The Lebanese army says that an Israeli airstrike killed two of its soldiers and wounded three others, an incident that further entangled Lebanon's official army in the escalating conflict between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Bassem Mroue, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize awarded to Japanese organization of atomic bombing survivors Nihon Hidankyo
item.description: Jørgen Watne Frydnes, chair of the Norwegian Nobel Committee, said the award was made as the “taboo against the use of nuclear weapon is under pressure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize has been awarded to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder And Elena Becatoros, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After a 2-year investigation, South African president won't be charged over $580K hidden in a couch
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa won't face criminal charges over a hidden cash scandal that was revealed more than two years ago and led to an investigation by a special police unit, prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China defiant over South China Sea skirmishes in ASEAN talks and blames meddling by foreign forces
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — Southeast Asian leaders stepped up pressure on China to respect international law following clashes in the disputed South China Sea, but Chinese Premier Li Qiang was defiant during annual summit talks on Thursday as he blamed “external forces” for interfering in regional affai
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG AND JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel seeks to reshape Middle East, with force and US backing
item.description: One year after Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack, Israel is reshaping the Middle East by force, taking the fight to Iran and its proxies with the help of the United States — despite pleas from the Biden administration for diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Israel's ground invasion in southern Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s ground invasion in Lebanon stretched into its second week, as the Hezbollah militant group fired hundreds of rockets deep into Israel — with no end in sight to the escalating conflict.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With roads often blocked by rebels in Congo, boats — the only alternative — have become death traps
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The overcrowded boat that capsized in eastern Congo last week killed eight members of Serge Nzonga’s family along with 70 others. Days later, he was back on the same route that claimed their lives in yet another boat lacking safety measures.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from an AP investigation of Russia recruiting Africans to make drones for use in Ukraine
item.description: About 200 women ages 18-22 from across Africa have been recruited to work in a factory alongside Russian vocational students assembling thousands of Iranian-designed attack drones to be launched into Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and LORI HINNANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, Netanyahu speak ahead of expected retaliation against Iran
item.description: President Biden spoke with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Wednesday for the first time in weeks, amid heightened tensions between the leaders as Israel calibrates its expected retaliatory strike against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: John Legend calls for Harris to put 'conditions' on US aid to Israel
item.description: Music superstar John Legend is calling for Vice President Harris to make U.S. aid to Israel conditional, amid its ongoing offensives in Gaza and Lebanon. In an interview with Zeteo’s Mehdi Hasan, Legend said America must use its aid to Israel as “leverage.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Nasrallah's replacement dead: ‘Thousands of terrorists’ killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the death of the Hezbollah leader slated to replace Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah and warned the Lebanese people to oust the terrorist group or endure more war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 4,000 items in the Staffordshire Hoard make it the largest collection in history of its kind
item.description: The Staffordshire Hoard is made of thousands of items. It was discovered by a farmer in 2009 and is the largest collection of its kind.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country’s history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and has since spiraled into wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: Iran’s missiles strike Israel, and a deadly terror attack in Tel Aviv
item.description: On the same day Iran attacked Israel with some 181 missiles last week, a terrorist attack saw 7 innocent Israelis murdered in cold blood by two Palestinians in the Tel Aviv area.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four American hostages remain in Hamas captivity a year after Oct. 7 massacre
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is vowing that the U.S. "will continue to work tirelessly" to bring home Americans being held hostage by Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: A strike on a mosque kills at least 19, Palestinian officials say, as Israel bombards northern Gaza and southern Lebanon
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on a mosque in the Gaza Strip early Sunday killed at least 19 people, Palestinian officials said, as Israel intensified its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Lebanon in a widening war with Iran-allied militant groups across the region.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
